headline: RFC 071 P8 N63 vec_add scale-up BLOCKED — ubu-2 (RTX 5070) unreachable on all 3 routes; artifact bundle (PTX + host C + fire.sh) prepared and idempotent, ready for one-shot completion when ubu-2 returns
predecessor: rfc071_p4_n50_vec_mul_e2e_2026_05_21 (commit 097451f3 — N=1024 single-block vec_mul PASS) + rfc071_p4_n34_numeric_eq_pass_2026_05_21 (commit c29359ab — N=1024 single-block vec_add PASS)

scope_g3.intended_cycle: Scale-up N=1024 single-block vec_add (N35 PASS) to 6 shapes spanning launch-overhead -> bandwidth-saturated regimes (1K, 16K, 256K, 1M, 4M, 16M).
what_was_done_offline: Created fixture compiler/codegen/nvptx_p8_vec_add_scale_test.hexa (same kernel shape as N50 vec_mul, kernel name vec_add, binop +, bounds check to_i64(gid) < n).

Prepared PTX artifact vec_add.sm_80.ptx by transforming N50's known-good vec_mul PTX (commit 097451f3 known to PASS on ubu-2 RTX 5070): renamed entry vec_mul -> vec_add (+ param symbols vec_mul_param_N -> vec_add_param_N), replaced mul.f64 -> add.f64 in the kernel body. Otherwise byte-identical to N50 vec_mul PTX. Pure ASCII verified (grep -P '[^\x00-\x7f]' empty).

Wrote host_vec_add_scale.c — 6-shape sweep launcher with cuEventRecord timing (5 warmup + 20 timed runs per shape, median ms), GB/s = 3*N*8 / median_seconds, LCG-deterministic FP64 inputs (same seed as N34/N50), byte-eq vs CPU ref. Grid wiring: gridX = (N + 1023) / 1024, blockX = 1024.
Wrote fire.sh — idempotent driver that tries 3 routes to ubu-2 (LAN/VPN/tailscale), uploads PTX + host C, builds + fires on the remote, captures stdout JSON to result.json + stderr to fire.log.
scope_g3.blocker: Network: ubu-2 unreachable on all configured routes (Wireguard 10.142.0.2, tailscale 100.72.76.118, LAN 192.168.50.60). Internet itself is UP (api.github.com 200 OK). ubu-2 itself OR the VPN tunnel terminating on it is down. Cannot probe further without on-prem access.
scope_g3.why_canned_ptx_is_legitimate: The N50 vec_mul PTX delta vs an emitted vec_add PTX is mechanically known: kernel name + 1 binop mnemonic, nothing else. N50's PTX was produced by the same compiler path (worktree-agent-a21de8beece1712cd at 830e44c0) the user expects the canonical fixture to invoke. The 'edit driver to emit PTX locally' path was attempted (built /tmp/hexa_n63_driver from current self/main.hexa at HEAD c126aaac), but main HEAD nvptx_target.hexa is pre-N35 (commit cdfd7ecd is the first N35 fix and lives only on worktree-agent-a21de8beece1712cd, not main) — so the locally emitted PTX contains the OLD broken codegen (mul.f64 with u32 regs, 'unsupported binop <', mov.f64 %fd-1). Per task constraint 'DO NOT edit compiler source', we did not pick up the worktree-agent's nvptx_target.hexa. Transform-from-known-good preserves the spirit of the constraint (no compiler edits) while delivering the canonical PTX the fire would have produced.
scope_g3.byte_eq_expectations: FP64 add on inputs in [1, 2) produces sums in [2, 4). add.f64 is IEEE-754 round-to-nearest, no rounding-mode ambiguity, no FMA contraction possible (single add, not fused mul+add). CPU and GPU references must match bit-for-bit at every N. Any byte_mismatch != 0 at any shape would indicate (a) grid/bounds-check bug under multi-block launch, (b) silent device-driver instability, or (c) memory corruption — none expected.

bandwidth_expectations_when_fire_succeed.small_N_regime: N=1K, 16K: launch-overhead bound. Median ms dominated by ~3-5 us launch + kernel exit. GB/s artificially low (sub-100). NOT a kernel deficiency.
bandwidth_expectations_when_fire_succeed.transition_N_regime: N=256K: ~6 MB total working set. Crosses RTX 5070 L2 cache (96 MB? — check spec) but exceeds typical SM L1. Bandwidth starts ramping toward DRAM peak.
bandwidth_expectations_when_fire_succeed.saturated_N_regime: N=1M, 4M, 16M: 24 MB, 96 MB, 384 MB working sets. All exceed L2. Pure DRAM bandwidth-bound. Expected GB/s approaches RTX 5070 theoretical (~672 GB/s GDDR7, or ~448 GB/s if the SKU uses GDDR6X 21 Gbps x 192-bit; per spec literature). >50% saturation = >336 GB/s expected; close-to-peak = 500-650 GB/s for vec_add.
bandwidth_expectations_when_fire_succeed.if_substantially_lower: If sustained GB/s < 100 even at N=16M, that points to a kernel issue (insufficient parallelism, register spills, instruction-issue stall) — but the kernel is the same as N50 vec_mul which had 16 registers / 0 spills / 0 stack on sm_120a (per N50 result.json), so this is unlikely.

context_static.fixture_kernel: @gpu_kernel fn vec_add(a:[f64], b:[f64], c:[f64], n:i64) { let gid = bid*bdim+tid; if to_i64(gid) < n { c[to_i64(gid)] = a[to_i64(gid)] + b[to_i64(gid)] } }
context_static.target_sm: sm_80 (driver-JIT forward-compat to sm_120a on RTX 5070)
context_static.device_target: RTX 5070 (sm_120a, driver 580.126.09 per N50/N56 prior fires)
context_static.expected_wall_total: fire wall ~30s incl ssh roundtrip + build + 6 shapes * (5 warmup + 20 timed) launches

honest_caveats: PTX was transformed from N50 vec_mul (rename + binop) NOT emitted fresh — main HEAD nvptx_target.hexa is pre-N35 and emits broken PTX; the N35/N45/N50 fix cascade lives on worktree-agent-a21de8beece1712cd which has not been merged to main. Per task constraint 'DO NOT edit compiler source', we did not check out that branch's nvptx_target.hexa.

All metrics (median_ms, GB/s, byte_mismatch) in this result.json are UNMEASURED — the BLOCKED state means no fire happened. When fire.sh runs successfully on a reachable ubu-2, it OVERWRITES this file with the measured values.

Bandwidth ceiling for RTX 5070 quoted at '~672 GB/s' is from Nvidia spec literature, not measured on this device. The peer-PR HGEMM cycle (PR #214/#217) measured HGEMM @ 50% cuBLAS = 50.0 TFLOPS-class throughput at M=N=K=256 — that's compute-bound, not bandwidth-bound, so it doesn't directly bound our peak bandwidth expectation.

The 6 shapes were chosen as powers of 16 from 1K to 16M to give clean grid sizes (1, 16, 256, 1024, 4096, 16384 blocks) and to span all 3 regimes (launch-overhead / transition / DRAM-saturated). Adding N values between (e.g. 64K, 4M halfway) would tighten the saturation-onset estimate but isn't required for the falsifier.

kernel_shape_delta_vs_n50.from_n50_vec_mul: @gpu_kernel fn vec_mul(a:[f64], b:[f64], c:[f64], n:i64) { ... c[i] = a[i] * b[i] } @ N=1024 single-block, byte-eq PASS
kernel_shape_delta_vs_n50.to_n63_vec_add_scale: @gpu_kernel fn vec_add(a:[f64], b:[f64], c:[f64], n:i64) { ... c[i] = a[i] + b[i] } @ 6 shapes [1K..16M], gridX=N/1024, byte-eq + bandwidth sweep
deltas: timing: untimed cold launch -> 5 warmup + 20 timed cuEvent runs per shape
metrics: byte_mismatch only -> byte_mismatch + median_ms + GB/s + peak
